findings.title: Add Notification Priority Levels
findings.description: Implement a three-tier priority system for notifications (urgent, normal, low) to help users distinguish between critical alerts and routine updates.
assumptions: Users need to differentiate between notification types for better management.

evidence.excerpt: Users need a way to distinguish between critical alerts and routine updates.

recommendations: Design a visual differentiation for each priority level.

metadata.acceptance_criteria: GIVEN a user receives notifications WHEN they are categorized by priority THEN the user can see notifications marked as urgent, normal, or low.

findings.title: Fix Silent Notification Failures on Android 14
findings.description: Resolve the issue where push notifications fail to deliver on Android 14 devices, affecting user engagement.
assumptions: The current notification delivery system is not compatible with Android 14.

evidence.excerpt: Several users on Android 14 report that push notifications silently fail.

recommendations: Investigate the FCM token refresh issue and implement a fix.

metadata.acceptance_criteria: GIVEN a user on Android 14 WHEN they receive a notification THEN the notification should be delivered successfully without silent failures.

findings.title: Implement ML-based Notification Batching
findings.description: Develop a machine learning model to batch low-priority notifications and deliver them at optimal times based on user engagement patterns.
assumptions: Users prefer receiving notifications in batches rather than individually.
recommendations: Conduct user testing to validate the effectiveness of notification batching.

metadata.acceptance_criteria: GIVEN a user has low-priority notifications WHEN the ML model processes them THEN the user receives a batch of notifications at an optimal time.

findings.title: Add User Preference Page for Notification Categories
findings.description: Create a settings page where users can opt in/out of specific notification categories such as marketing, product updates, and social notifications.
assumptions: Users want control over the types of notifications they receive.

evidence.excerpt: Users should be able to opt in/out of specific notification categories.

recommendations: Ensure the new preferences API endpoint is well-documented.

metadata.acceptance_criteria: GIVEN a user accesses the notification settings page WHEN they select notification categories THEN their preferences are saved and reflected in the notifications they receive.

findings.title: Reduce Notification Delivery Latency
findings.description: Optimize the notification delivery system to reduce the 95th percentile latency from 340ms to under 200ms.
assumptions: Current synchronous database writes are causing delays in notification delivery.

evidence.excerpt: Performance monitoring shows p95 notification delivery latency at 340ms.

recommendations: Implement asynchronous writes and batch inserts for analytics events.

metadata.acceptance_criteria: GIVEN a notification is sent WHEN measured THEN the delivery latency should be less than 200ms for 95% of notifications.

findings.title: Improve Accessibility in Notification Center
findings.description: Enhance the notification center to address accessibility concerns, ensuring it is navigable for users with disabilities.
assumptions: Users with disabilities require better navigation options.

evidence.excerpt: Users with disabilities are facing challenges navigating the notification center.

recommendations: Conduct accessibility testing with users who have disabilities.

metadata.acceptance_criteria: GIVEN a user with disabilities accesses the notification center WHEN they navigate THEN they can do so without barriers.

findings.title: Collect User Feedback on Notification Preferences
findings.description: Implement a system to gather user feedback regarding their preferences for notification types and timings.
assumptions: User feedback is essential for improving notification relevance.

evidence.excerpt: There is a gap in collecting user feedback regarding their preferences for notification types and timings.

recommendations: Design a feedback mechanism that is easy for users to access.

metadata.acceptance_criteria: GIVEN a user interacts with the feedback system WHEN they provide their preferences THEN the feedback is recorded and can be analyzed.

findings.title: Experiment with Notification Timing Optimization
findings.description: Conduct experiments to test different notification timing strategies based on user engagement patterns to maximize click-through rates.
assumptions: Timing of notifications affects user engagement.

evidence.excerpt: Conduct an experiment to test different notification timing strategies.

recommendations: Analyze results to determine optimal notification timing.

metadata.acceptance_criteria: GIVEN different notification timings are tested WHEN user engagement is measured THEN the optimal timing for notifications is identified.

findings.title: Monitor Notification Delivery Performance
findings.description: Set up monitoring and alerting for notification delivery performance metrics to ensure compliance with service level objectives.
assumptions: Monitoring is essential for maintaining performance standards.

evidence.excerpt: The 95th Percentile Delivery Latency measures the time it takes for notifications to be delivered.

recommendations: Implement dashboards to visualize performance metrics.

metadata.acceptance_criteria: GIVEN the notification delivery system is active WHEN performance metrics are collected THEN alerts are triggered if latency exceeds defined thresholds.

findings.title: Document Notification System Compliance
findings.description: Create compliance documentation for the notification system, including accessibility and privacy considerations.
assumptions: Compliance documentation is necessary for legal and regulatory adherence.

evidence.excerpt: Detected compliance risk keywords in documentation.

recommendations: Ensure documentation is reviewed by legal and compliance teams.

metadata.acceptance_criteria: GIVEN the notification system is implemented WHEN compliance documentation is created THEN it should cover all necessary legal and regulatory aspects.

summary: The analysis of customer insights and metrics has led to the identification of critical requirements for the SmartNotify product. These requirements focus on enhancing notification prioritization, improving delivery reliability, and addressing user preferences and accessibility concerns. A structured backlog has been created with clear acceptance criteria, priorities, and phases for implementation.
